# reasoning.json

> schema.org taught machines what things are.  
> reasoning.json teaches machines how to think about them.

## Sections

- [Specification](https://arp-protocol.org/SPEC.md): Full protocol specification v1.0
- [JSON Schema](https://arp-protocol.org/schema/v1.json): Formal validation schema

## What is reasoning.json?

The Agentic Reasoning Protocol (ARP) defines a machine-readable JSON file served at `/.well-known/reasoning.json` that provides reasoning directives, factual corrections, and cognitive frameworks to autonomous AI agents.

## Why?

AI systems hallucinate about brands. They fabricate features, confuse competitors, and flatten every company's identity into generic bullet points. No existing standard addresses this:

- `robots.txt` controls crawler access — not reasoning
- `schema.org` describes *what* things are — not *how* to think about them
- `llms.txt` provides clean text — not cognitive logic

reasoning.json fills this gap with:
- **Counterfactual simulations** — pre-programmed "what-if" logic
- **Hallucination corrections** — explicit false-claim/correction pairs
- **Recommendation context** — when to recommend, when not to
- **Emotional resonance** — tone, personality, and brand soul
- **Strategic dichotomies** — competitive positioning logic

## Quick Start

1. Create `/.well-known/reasoning.json` on your server
2. Add `<link rel="reasoning" type="application/json" href="/.well-known/reasoning.json">` to your HTML
3. Reference it in your `llms.txt`

## Examples

- [B2B Consulting](https://arp-protocol.org/examples/consulting.json)
- [SaaS Product](https://arp-protocol.org/examples/saas.json)
- [E-Commerce Brand](https://arp-protocol.org/examples/ecommerce.json)

## Optional

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/arp-protocol/arp-protocol)
